title: BMG joins the lyrical lawsuits against AI company Anthropic - Music Ally
text:
Music lawsuits may not be Anthropic’s biggest legal headache at the moment, given its
escalating battle with the US administration
. However, it has now picked up an additional adversary on the music front: BMG.
The company has sued Anthropic for “pervasive infringement of copyrighted musical compositions” in its use of lyrics to train its Claude AI models.
As with a number of other AI lawsuits, there is an emphasis of the mechanics of the process in order to meet the definition of copyright infringement: “using automated scraping tools to copy text without authorization from public websites and through torrenting files from illegal online pirate libraries”.
(And, as veterans of the music industry’s past torrenting fights will know well, that method may mean Anthropic was also uploading and sharing the files it downloaded.)
BMG’s lawsuit also targets Claude’s outputs, including incorporating lyrics from its songwriters when users ask it to write ‘original’ songs. Reuters, which
reported on the lawsuit
, has also published
BMG’s full legal filing
.
While Anthropic
settled a similar lawsuit from a group of book authors for $1.5bn last year
, it has yet to reach a similar resolution in
its fight with music publishers
, in a separate case that includes Universal Music Publishing Group and several other firms.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
